answer:
\boxed{F}

If the Sun collapsed into a black hole, Earth would **not** be consumed by a supernova (which only occurs in massive stars) or freeze immediately—it would instead **likely be sucked into the black hole** over time due to gravitational forces, though tidal forces (option H) would also play a role. The Sun’s mass would remain nearly the same, so Earth’s orbit would not be stable in the new extreme gravitational field. The most accurate single choice is **F**.
